Off Calendar Aug-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Dirk Van Ausdall for plaintiff and moving party: John P. Murray
Craig W. Wallace for defendant and opposing party: Stefan Giustino

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with deposition to plaintiff and defendant to resume by August 26, 2005: Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to deposition questions is granted in part. The motion is granted except as to questions 142.19 and 145.17. While defendant's previous motion to quash was granted in part, the Court did not find that discovery was strictly limited to the subject property. Rather, the motion addressed subpoenas that as drafted were overbroad and sought production of either privileged or irrelevant information. The knowledge and experience of the witness are relevant to the claims set forth in the complaint.

The motion for monetary sanctions is denied as defendant objected to certain questions based upon a good faith interpretation of the prior order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Off Calendar Jun-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES
Dirk Van Ausdall for plaintiff and moving party.
TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to quash employment records subpoena is granted in part and denied in part. 1 - 2: Granted in part. The subpoena is modified to only require production of a list of the number of transactions by year. Any information that identifies the address, the lot, parcel or block number of the specified property, the names of the parties to the transaction, or any other information that identifies either the property or persons or entities participating in the transaction shall be redacted.
3 - 6:
Granted in part. The subpoena is modified to only require production of a list of individual commission dollar amounts by year. Any information that identifies the address, the lot, parcel or block number of the specified property, the names of the parties to the transaction, or any other information that identifies either the property or persons or entities participating in the transaction shall be redacted.
7:
The motion is granted as the request is overbroad.
8:
The motion is denied.
9 - 10:
The motion is granted. The request seeks information irrelevant to the subject matter of this action.
All requests for monetary sanctions are denied.
Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
